commit.message:
feat: expand semantic indexing and managed project workflows

Add Drupal and Symfony adapters with project type detection, improve managed remote project caching and branch scan execution, and extend semantic indexing for Twig, SDC, Drupal libraries, and YAML-backed relations so the UI and search flows can link assets, services, and project symbols more accurately.

Key improvements:
- Improved SDC component indexing with automatic asset linking (Twig, CSS, JS).
- Added Drupal 7 support to VersionDetector via .info file parsing.
- Enhanced branch management UI with last scan status and rescan actions.
- Fixed ImportCommand to correctly parse DMU upgrade patterns.
- Modernized test suite with PHP 8 attributes and tighter Twig grammar requirements.
- Optimized remote project storage by moving repositories to .cache/ directory.

Co-authored-by: Gemini CLI <gemini-cli@google.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: derive task deps from spec dependencies

Resolve direct spec depends_on relationships during derive by linking to active derived tasks when they exist, and warn without blocking when they do not. Adds regression coverage for both paths while preserving existing parent/child dependency behavior.

Task: @01KGVGSH49ACMXJBGCYW12ZDYF
Spec: @cmd-derive
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(settings): harden error typing and settings copy behavior

Summary:
- replace fragile settings handler error routing with typed errors via
  `ErrUserNotFound` and `ValidationError`
- switch pause multiplier updates to per-field pointer semantics so omitted
  values are distinct from explicit values
- align settings copy with runtime behavior and add backend/web tests plus
  runtime media artifacts for validation

Rationale:
- string matching on error text is brittle and can silently misroute status
  codes when upstream wording changes
- pause multiplier updates should not rely on `0.0` as a missing sentinel
  because explicit values and omission are different API intents
- UI copy should match how font size is applied immediately while other
  settings are primarily defaults for new documents

Tests:
- cd backend && "$(go env GOPATH)/bin/go1.24.0" test ./internal/settings
- pnpm --filter @speed-reader/web prebuild
- pnpm --filter @speed-reader/web test -- --run SettingsView
- runtime walkthrough media captured in docs/pr-media/SPE-6

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(extension): harden webstore build and packaging readiness

Summary:
- Added an extension prebuild step that compiles required workspace
  packages before running the extension build.
- Added a deterministic extension package script that zips the built
  dist output using the manifest version in the filename.
- Removed external Google Fonts links from the sidepanel HTML and added
  a Chrome Web Store readiness matrix with policy/source mapping.

Rationale:
- The extension build failed from a clean workspace because shared
  package artifacts were missing, which blocked a reliable release path.
- Production submission requires a reproducible upload artifact and clear
  requirement coverage for Web Store review fields and policy checks.
- Avoiding external font URLs reduces review/runtime risk under MV3
  remote code expectations.

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @speed-reader/types clean && pnpm --filter
  @speed-reader/tokenizer clean && pnpm --filter @speed-reader/engine
  clean && pnpm --filter @speed-reader/api-client clean && pnpm
  --filter @speed-reader/ui clean
- pnpm --filter @speed-reader/extension build
- pnpm --filter @speed-reader/extension package
- ZIP/manifest sanity check for generated package contents

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Make worksheet regeneration dynamic, regenerate .tst files (#329)

Detect worksheet inputs by scanning tst/worksheets for non-empty
.sheet directories instead of hardcoding a short list. This lets
regen_tests.g create or refresh expected output for all runnable
worksheet fixtures.

Print a brief progress line before each worksheet so long
regeneration runs show which sheet is being processed.

Also let regen_test.g regenerate the .tst files as well.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Sync sprint notes with slice completion.

Update README/architecture/sprint/roadmap docs for the shipping flow.

Add --list-reasons to capability audit and surface an explained skip-reason catalog (code, count, description). Improve parse failures with explicit error/hint messaging, keep reason filter normalization deterministic, and extend audit tests/docs/sprint notes.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

tooling(capability-audit): add reason/threshold drilldown filters

Extend homey:capability-audit with --min-skip-signals and --reason filters for runtime-weighted capability expansion targeting. Add runtimeFilterSignals to ranking rows, expose runtime filter state in summary/markdown output, update README/sprint docs, and add parser + behavior regression coverage.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Simplify hashset semantics and consolidate ADT test coverage

- remove unused deletion/tombstone bookkeeping from hash_set and keep the
  API focused on non-deleting open addressing
- enforce non-zero bucket initialization and keep resize policy strictly
  growth-based to avoid ambiguous shrink behavior
- keep probing style consistent with for-loop increment expressions in all
  hashset users while preserving resize-on-insert behavior
- refactor symbol table insertion flow to probe first, then resize and
  restart iteration state in-place when capacity must grow
- centralize generic FNV helpers in include/pycomparse/adt/hash.h for shared
  string hashing, and keep object-type-specific hashing local to
  object_intern.c
- merge arena_test and hashset_test into a single adt_test executable built
  from src/adt_test.c, and remove the legacy standalone arena_test source

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
perf: optimize indexing, benchmark cleanup, and safe Swoole configuration

Indexing Optimization:
- Implemented a parent-owned SQLite merge strategy for parallel indexing. Workers now
  extract symbol payloads and serialize them using igbinary to temporary files,
  avoiding SQLite database lock contention during concurrent writes.
- The parent process performs a single merged transaction, improving indexing
  throughput on larger Drupal trees while keeping symbol counts stable.
- Optimized FFI handling by ensuring FFIBinding is fork-aware and refreshes
  automatically on PID change, removing redundant explicit resets.

Benchmarks & Documentation:
- Removed stale benchmark snapshots and kept the maintained storage and baseline
  indexing benchmark scripts.
- Stopped the baseline benchmark from writing tracked JSON output by default and
  documented the current benchmark workflow and findings.
- Added ignore rules for generated benchmark result files.

Swoole & Concurrency:
- Added DrupalEvolver\SwooleConfig to detect restricted environments where
  io_uring_setup() is blocked, preventing engine-level crashes.
- Documented that pcntl_fork remains the superior model for CPU-bound FFI parsing
  tasks compared to Swoole coroutine mode or AMPHP process IPC.
- Removed redundant Swoole and AMPHP indexer implementations after benchmarking
  confirmed they were unsuitable for this workload.

Infrastructure:
- Added IndexingPerformanceTest to enforce a minimum baseline of 200 files/sec.
- Updated DatabaseApi::getStats() to provide unified file and symbol counts.
- Synced Docker, composer metadata, and CLI bootstrap with the current runtime.

Co-authored-by: Gemini CLI <gemini-cli@google.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Add --focus actionable|all (default actionable) to homey:capability-audit. When runtime support diagnostics are present, actionable focus now filters ranking to capabilities with real skip signals and reports top skip reasons globally and per capability row. Update README and sprint docs, and expand audit parser/behavior tests.

Add --noise-filter actionable|all to inspect and inspect-live (default actionable).\nSuppress technical-only unmatched rows from top-unmatched diagnostics in list/markdown/ndjson while preserving full output with --noise-filter all.\nCover parser and behavior with focused tests and document the new flag in README.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(workspace): make migration and service compatible with merged auth setup

Replace unsupported Liquibase addCheckConstraint usage with SQL constraints and load WorkspaceService only when WorkspaceRepository is present (mock profile excludes JPA).

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Improve compile benchmark runners and default CPU affinity control

- add a `callgrind` runner mode that measures instruction counts (`Ir`)
  by running each engine once under Valgrind Callgrind
- ignore warmup/repeat sampling in callgrind mode and warn when users
  explicitly pass `--warmup` or `--repeats`
- adjust table and totals output for callgrind to use `Ir` units
- propagate metric units into TSV headers and JSON metadata
- add in-script CPU affinity control via `--cpu-affinity`
- enable affinity by default with `auto` selection
- implement topology-aware auto affinity using `lscpu` data:
  choose an online high-frequency CPU, avoid cpu0's sibling core when
  possible, and prefer a primary SMT thread
- support `--cpu-affinity off` to disable pinning and manual CPU set
  syntax such as `2` and `2,4-5`

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

phase7: deweight technical unmatched noise in expansion-candidate scoring

Expansion-candidate ranking now ignores unmatched pressure when signals are technical-only (high-unmatched-ratio / suppressed-fill-actions) and profile outcome is otherwise stable, avoiding inflated scores for curated triples.

Adds regression coverage and syncs sprint/roadmap notes.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Refactor arena sizing to size_t and rename tuple constant helper

- rename ast_tuple_compute_constant to ast_tuple_as_constant in the
  public header and all call sites
- migrate arena grow APIs and internal size bookkeeping to size_t:
  block header size, arena allocation offsets, grow offsets, and block
  allocation sizing
- update scanner, unparse, and codegen call sites to use size_t-based
  arena APIs and add explicit bounds checks at uint32_t/unsigned
  boundaries
- keep narrowing explicit where downstream object and bytecode metadata
  remains 32-bit

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
🔧 config(deploy): 迁移 vercel 部署配置到 v1 CLI 方案

将项目的 vercel 部署入口切换为 `vercel-deploy-tool deploy`，
并新增根级 `vercel-deploy-tool.config.ts` 作为 v1 配置文件。

本次迁移同步改用 `defineConfig` 和 `vercelProjectName`，
保留现有部署目标，并兼容通过环境变量覆盖 Vercel 凭据。

commit.message:
homey: cascade-delete node devices when a bridge is removed

- add app runtime helper deleteNodeDevicesForBridge() to remove node devices bound to a bridgeId via manager/devices API

- wire bridge device onDeleted() to run cascade delete before tearing down bridge connection

- keep bridge deletion resilient: partial cascade failures are logged and bridge teardown still proceeds

- add app-runtime + bridge-device harness tests for cascade behavior and partial-failure reporting

- sync architecture and sprint docs with bridge lifecycle cascade semantics

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

homey: close phase 6 and polish settings diagnostics refresh UX

- extend settings runtime presenter with diagnostics refresh status/last success/last failure/last reason rows

- surface failure reason as runtime hint when no actionable recommendations are present

- add presenter regression coverage for diagnostics refresh rows and failure hint behavior

- mark Phase 6 complete and sync README/architecture/sprint/roadmap for Phase 7 kickoff

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

homey: surface per-bridge diagnostics refresh telemetry

- track bridge diagnostics refresh success/failure markers in app runtime and expose them via runtime diagnostics + bridge inventory APIs

- render refresh timestamps/reason in settings configured-bridges table and bridge tools runtime/summary panels

- add transition coverage to ensure refresh failure metadata clears after a later successful refresh

- sync architecture/API contract/sprint/roadmap docs for the telemetry slice

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(agents): merge workflow git and pr rules

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

更新项目协作约定与速记

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

homey: fail open settings bootstrap when request gate script is missing

- replace hard bootstrap return with local in-page request-order gate fallback

- keep diagnostics/settings UI operational even when shared request-order gate asset is unavailable

- add guard assertions to prevent future fail-closed bootstrap regressions

- sync architecture and sprint notes for the remediation slice

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

homey: harden panel handler callback paths and event contracts

- extend panel liveness gate with callback-mode coverage across active pair/repair handlers

- add callback timeout/error propagation checks for stalled and invalid-action paths

- lock timed-session handler event inventory per driver to prevent panel contract drift

- sync architecture and sprint/roadmap docs for the hardening slices

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: productionize app with security, performance, and CI improvements

Summary:
- Enable Content Security Policy (CSP) in Tauri config (was null/disabled)
- Disable right-click context menu in production builds
- Wrap SortableTodoItem, ProgressBar, FilterTabs with React.memo
- Memoize derived state (filteredTodos, completedCount) with useMemo
- Enable logging in production builds at Warn level (was debug-only)
- Add lint and test jobs to CI workflow before platform builds
- Upload build artifacts per platform in CI
- Fill in Cargo.toml metadata (license, repo, description)

Rationale:
- CSP null is a critical security gap for a production Tauri app
- React.memo/useMemo reduce unnecessary re-renders as todo lists grow
- CI should gate on lint+test before expensive cross-platform builds
- Production logging at Warn level aids debugging without noise

Tests:
- vitest run: 30/30 passed
- eslint: clean
- vite build: success

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

新增吸积盘 V2 设计文档

homey: add panel liveness gate for pair and repair handlers

- add table-driven liveness contract tests for active panel events across bridge/node pair and repair flows

- assert response-shape expectations and per-handler invocation deadlines to catch spinner/hang regressions

- add timeout-behavior regression checks to ensure timed session wrappers reject stalled handlers

- sync architecture and sprint/roadmap docs for the new liveness gate coverage

commit.message:
homey: harden settings against stale diagnostics responses

- add request-order gate module for browser pages (settings/request-order-gate)

- ignore stale diagnostics/inventory responses when newer requests are in flight

- disable/re-enable Bridge Scope and Refresh controls deterministically during in-flight requests

- add request-order gate regression tests and timeout-guard assertions

- sync architecture and sprint/roadmap docs for this reliability slice

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(workspace): add workspace and membership domain model

Implements issue #111 with Liquibase schema, JPA entities, repositories, service lookups, and repository tests for membership queries and workspace delete cascade.

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

tools: add bridge-scoped flags to runtime smoke and support bundle CLIs

- add optional --bridge-id to homey:runtime-api:smoke and homey:support-bundle

- forward bridgeId to runtime diagnostics/support/recommendation routes where supported

- include bridge filter metadata in support-bundle exports (json/markdown source metadata)

- extend core tool tests for CLI parsing and query/body propagation

- sync README, API contract, architecture, sprint, and roadmap docs

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>
